perturbation.original_text: Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate ------------------ driver appearances (2) two-hours each at no fee. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. Additional appearances by Sabco drivers shall be at the discounted rate of [ * ] ($[ * ]) Dollars per day per driver, [ * ]. Sabco shall further provide the services of each driver for one free day for commercial use (up to six hours each) for still photo shoot, TV and radio/commercial production. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, shall allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate ------------------ driver appearances (2) two-hours each at no fee, subject to driver availability. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. Additional appearances by Sabco drivers may be provided at Sabco's sole discretion, with rates to be determined at the time of the request. Sabco shall further provide the services of each driver for one free day for commercial use (up to six hours each) for still photo shoot, TV and radio/commercial production; driver availability for these commercial uses is not guaranteed. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, may or may not allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement; approval is subject to each driver’s individual agreement and cannot be guaranteed by Sabco.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty and contradiction regarding driver appearances. The original text states that Sabco 'shall provide' five driver appearances and one free day for commercial use, and that drivers 'shall allow' their likeness to be used for endorsements 'at no additional costs'. The modified text introduces phrases like 'subject to driver availability,' 'may be provided at Sabco's sole discretion,' 'driver availability for these commercial uses is not guaranteed,' and 'may or may not allow,' thus undermining the initial mandatory obligation. This contradicts the original clause where Sabco was responsible to provide the services of each driver and now their presence depends on the drivers approval. The insertion of 'may or may not allow' directly clashes with the original text's statement that drivers 'shall allow' their likeness to be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Driver Appearances. Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate ------------------ driver appearances (2) two-hours each at no fee, subject to driver availability. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. Additional appearances by Sabco drivers may be provided at Sabco's sole discretion, with rates to be determined at the time of the request. Sabco shall further provide the services of each driver for one free day for commercial use (up to six hours each) for still photo shoot, TV and radio/commercial production; driver availability for these commercial uses is not guaranteed. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, may or may not allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement; approval is subject to each driver’s individual agreement and cannot be guaranteed by Sabco.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Sabco to provide driver appearances and endorsements, while the changed text introduces uncertainty by using phrases like 'subject to driver availability,' 'may be provided at Sabco's sole discretion,' and 'may or may not allow.' This directly contradicts the initial mandatory obligations, making the fulfillment of these services uncertain and subject to driver approval. This uncertainty directly clashes with the original text's statement that drivers 'shall allow' their likeness to be used, creating a contradiction. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed text, and inherits this contradiction, therefore the correct response is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Exclusivity. During the term of this Agreement, Sabco will not ----------- represent or accept as a primary, secondary, or associate sponsor any other company reasonably deemed to be competitive with Prolong products.
perturbation.changed_text: Exclusivity. During the term of this Agreement, Sabco will not ----------- represent or accept as a primary sponsor any other company reasonably deemed to be competitive with Prolong products. Sabco reserves the right to engage with secondary or associate sponsors, even if they offer similar products or services, provided that such engagements do not directly undermine the benefits provided to Prolong as the primary sponsor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text of Section 7 provides a broad exclusivity clause, preventing Sabco from representing any sponsor competitive with Prolong at any level (primary, secondary, or associate). The modified text weakens this exclusivity by stating Sabco can engage with secondary or associate sponsors that offer similar products as long as it doesn't directly 'undermine' Prolong's benefits. This creates a contradiction and ambiguity: what constitutes 'directly undermine'? This also contradicts the the first sentence of the same clause where Sabco will not represent or accept 'any other company' at any sponsorship level. This change introduces uncertainty about the extent of Prolong's exclusivity, and Sabco now has the flexibility to engage with competitors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exclusivity. During the term of this Agreement, Sabco will not ----------- represent or accept as a primary sponsor any other company reasonably deemed to be competitive with Prolong products. Sabco reserves the right to engage with secondary or associate sponsors, even if they offer similar products or services, provided that such engagements do not directly undermine the benefits provided to Prolong as the primary sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that Sabco cannot represent any company competitive with Prolong at any level (primary, secondary, or associate). The changed text now allows Sabco to engage with secondary or associate sponsors that offer similar products, creating an ambiguity and contradicting the initial broad exclusivity. The contradicted text restates the changed text. Since the changed text contradicts the original text, and the contradicted text is the changed text itself, then there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Prolong agrees to pay unto Sabco the sum [ * ] [ * ] ------------ Dollars ($[ * ]) for services to be provided by Sabco over this three year period of 1998, 1999 and year-2000. This sponsorship fee of $ [ * ] shall be paid to Sabco in the following installments: Year One (1998): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/98, 6/15/98, 8/15/98 and 10/15/98); Year Two (1999): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/99, 6/15/99, 8/15/99 and 10/15/99); Year Three (year-2000): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/2000, 6/15/2000,8/15/2000 and 10/15/2000).
perturbation.changed_text: Prolong agrees to pay unto Sabco the sum [ * ] [ * ] ------------ Dollars ($[ * ]) for services to be provided by Sabco over this three year period of 1998, 1999 and year-2000, subject to Sabco's full compliance with all material terms of this agreement. This sponsorship fee of $ [ * ] shall be paid to Sabco in the following installments: Year One (1998): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/98, 6/15/98, 8/15/98 and 10/15/98, provided Sabco has met all performance milestones as defined in Exhibit A); Year Two (1999): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/99, 6/15/99, 8/15/99 and 10/15/99, contingent upon satisfactory performance in the previous year); Year Three (year-2000): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/2000, 6/15/2000,8/15/2000 and 10/15/2000, based on mutual agreement of continued success of the partnership).
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 4 clearly defines the compensation and payment schedule. By adding 'subject to Sabco's full compliance' and conditional clauses regarding performance milestones, satisfactory performance, and mutual agreement, the modified text creates uncertainty. The term 'shall' has been changed to conditional compliance by Sabco. This change directly contradicts the original agreement. It makes enforcement unclear, as Prolong can now argue for non-payment based on subjective assessments of compliance and performance. Also, the insertion of 'subject to Sabco's full compliance with all material terms of this agreement', impacts the first sentence where Prolong agrees to pay, thus creating in-text contradiction due to the lack of certainty to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation. Prolong agrees to pay unto Sabco the sum [ * ] [ * ] ------------ Dollars ($[ * ]) for services to be provided by Sabco over this three year period of 1998, 1999 and year-2000, subject to Sabco's full compliance with all material terms of this agreement. This sponsorship fee of $ [ * ] shall be paid to Sabco in the following installments: Year One (1998): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/98, 6/15/98, 8/15/98 and 10/15/98, provided Sabco has met all performance milestones as defined in Exhibit A); Year Two (1999): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/99, 6/15/99, 8/15/99 and 10/15/99, contingent upon satisfactory performance in the previous year); Year Three (year-2000): $ [ * ] (four equal installments of $ [ * ] to be paid on 3/15/2000, 6/15/2000,8/15/2000 and 10/15/2000, based on mutual agreement of continued success of the partnership).
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a payment schedule with the word 'shall,' indicating a clear obligation. The changed text introduces conditional clauses such as 'subject to Sabco's full compliance,' 'provided Sabco has met all performance milestones,' 'contingent upon satisfactory performance,' and 'based on mutual agreement.' These additions transform the payment obligation from a fixed commitment to one dependent on subjective assessments of compliance and performance. This creates a direct contradiction because the 'shall' in the original agreement implies certainty and obligation, whereas the conditional clauses introduce uncertainty and potential for non-payment, thus causing an in-text contradiction between the payment obligation in the original text and the contradicted text. This satisfies the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because it alters the payment clause in a way that creates conflicting implications.
